heading: Sanctions with respect to foreign financial institutions that provide financial services to certain sanctioned persons
label: 9221b Sanctions with respect to foreign financial institutions that provide financial services to certain sanctioned persons

an applicable United Nations Security Council resolution.

children.heading: Asset blocking
children.label: 1 Asset blocking
children.text: The Secretary may block and prohibit, pursuant to the International Emergency Economic Powers Act ( et seq.), all transactions in all property and interests in property of the foreign financial institution if such property and interests in property are in the United States, come within the United States, or are or come within the possession or control of a United States person.50 U.S.C. 1701

children.heading: Restrictions on correspondent and payable-through accounts
children.label: 2 Restrictions on correspondent and payable-through accounts
children.text: The Secretary may prohibit, or impose strict conditions on, the opening or maintaining in the United States of a correspondent account or a payable-through account by the foreign financial institution.

children.heading: Implementation
children.label: 1 Implementation
children.text: The President may exercise all authorities provided under sections 203 and 205 of the International Emergency Economic Powers Act ( and 1704) to carry out this section.50 U.S.C. 1702

children.heading: Penalties
children.label: 2 Penalties
children.text: A person that violates, attempts to violate, conspires to violate, or causes a violation of this section or any regulation, license, or order issued to carry out this section shall be subject to the penalties set forth in subsections (b) and (c) of section 206 of the International Emergency Economic Powers Act () to the same extent as a person that commits an unlawful act described in subsection (a) of that section.50 U.S.C. 1705

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: d Regulations
children.text: Not later than 120 days after , the President shall, as appropriate, prescribe regulations to carry out this section.December 20, 2019

children.heading: Exception relating to importation of goods
children.label: e Exception relating to importation of goods

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Notwithstanding or any provision of this section, the authorities and requirements to impose sanctions under this section shall not include the authority or a requirement to impose sanctions on the importation of goods.section 9254(b) of this title

children.heading: Good defined
children.label: 2 Good defined
children.text: In this subsection, the term “good” means any article, natural or manmade substance, material, supply or manufactured product, including inspection and test equipment, and excluding technical data.

children.heading: Account; correspondent account; payable-through account
children.label: 1 Account; correspondent account; payable-through account
children.text: The terms “account”, “correspondent account”, and “payable-through account” have the meanings given those terms in .section 5318A of title 31

children.heading: Foreign financial institution
children.label: 2 Foreign financial institution
children.text: The term “foreign financial institution” has the meaning given that term in section 510.309 of title 31, Code of Federal Regulations (or any corresponding similar regulation or ruling).

children.heading: Knowingly
children.label: 3 Knowingly
children.text: The term “knowingly”, with respect to conduct, a circumstance, or a result, means that a person has actual knowledge, or should have known, of the conduct, the circumstance, or the result.
